workflow.name: Track SaaS spend from email invoices with AgentMail, JigsawStack and Postgres
workflow.description:
## WHAT you need 

1. Jigsaw API key for image processing, I use this as a gatekeeper/second pair of eyes.  
3. LINK to their website
 https://jigsawstack.com/
6. SECOND A postgress DATABASE (I use [Supabase](Supabase))
8. [LlamaCloud](LlamaCloud) for the parsing (you can replace this with Jigsaw if you like)
9. Redis (Optional , but I would strongly advise using it)
10. [agentMail](agentMail) , this is your trigger email adress and BOX
11. Webhook Trigger ([Svix](Svix) or n8n's builtin webhook)

## WHAT is it? 
This is an easy way to track your SaaS spending, especially for small business owners or solopreneurs. It's essentially exactly what tools like Spendbase and Ramp do, without the virtual card.

## HOW it works 
After getting your API keys, all you need to do is set up an AgentMail inbox for your invoices. This is your trigger.

In the current setup, the workflow will only trigger if you send an attachment.

You can find more information on how to use the workflow in the workflow notes.

## The database schema
You can find it as a deactivated code node in the workflow.

## Free 15 min Onboarding Coffee 
 
If you want I can walk you through the setup and you will have it up and running in no time.
[Free onboarding chat](https://app.reclaim.ai/m/n8n-onboarding/n8n)

parameters.content:
## SpendBoss

### How it works

1. Receives email attachments via a webhook for processing.
2. Verifies the type of document and evaluates the next steps.
3. Routes the document for parsing and storing the results.
4. Handles successful completion and logging of operations.
5. Includes error handling and waits before retrying failed tasks.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure AgentMail Webhook URL.
- [ ] Set up access to JigsawStack API for document checking.
- [ ] Ensure Postgres database credentials are provided for data storage.
- [ ] Make sure you have the necessary permissions for the sub-workflow 'parseInvoice'.
- [ ] Set appropriate batch sizes and wait durations.

### Customization

Batch sizes and waiting times can be adjusted to optimize processing speed and resource utilization.

## Initial email handling

Starts with an AgentMail webhook and checks attachments.

## Pipeline job acquisition

Handles acquiring and verifying pipeline jobs from the attachment.

## Job processing decision

Split items based on acquisition results and prepare skipped logs.

## Document verification and processing

Checks if the document is an invoice and evaluates the next steps.

## Invoice parsing and database operation

Parses invoices and upserts data into the database.

## SQL error handling

Handles SQL errors and sets events to wait before retries.

## Completion and logging

Completes the job, logs success, and waits if needed.

options.queryReplacement: ={{ JSON.stringify({ job_key: "attachment:" + ($json.attachment_id || "unknown"), attachment_id: $json.attachment_id || null, payload: $json, workflow_name: "SpendBase Inbound", stage: "attachment_received" }) }}

parameters.query:
DO
$body$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_type t
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = t.typnamespace
    WHERE t.typname = 'billing_type_enum' AND n.nspname = 'public'
  ) THEN
    CREATE TYPE public.billing_type_enum AS ENUM (
      'recurring',
      'one_time',
      'usage_based',
      'penalty'
    );
  END IF;
END
$body$;

-- 1. Create the Table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.pipeline_audit_logs (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    source text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'n8n',
    workflow_name text NULL,
    node_name text NULL,
    execution_id text NULL,
    fingerprint text NULL,
    attachment_id text NULL,
    document_id uuid NULL REFERENCES public.documents(id),
    vendor_id uuid NULL REFERENCES public.vendors(id),
    attempt integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    stage text NOT NULL CHECK (length(TRIM(stage)) > 0),
    status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info' CHECK (status = ANY (ARRAY['info','success','warning','error'])),
    message text NULL,
    payload jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    error jsonb NULL,
    context jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb
);

-- 2. Create Indexes
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs_fingerprint_idx ON public.pipeline_audit_logs (fingerprint);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs_execution_id_idx ON public.pipeline_audit_logs (execution_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs_created_at_idx ON public.pipeline_audit_logs (created_at DESC);

-- 3. Create the Function (The part that was missing!)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.log_pipeline_event(p_event jsonb)
RETURNS uuid LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER AS $body$
DECLARE v_log_id uuid;
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO public.pipeline_audit_logs (
        source, workflow_name, node_name, execution_id, fingerprint, 
        attachment_id, document_id, vendor_id, stage, status, 
        message, payload, error, context
    ) VALUES (
        coalesce(p_event->>'source', 'n8n'),
        p_event->>'workflowName',
        p_event->>'nodeName',
        p_event->>'executionId',
        p_event->>'fingerprint',
        p_event->>'attachmentId',
        (p_event->>'documentId')::uuid,
        (p_event->>'vendorId')::uuid,
        p_event->>'stage',
        coalesce(p_event->>'status', 'info'),
        p_event->>'message',
        coalesce(p_event->'payload', '{}'::jsonb),
        p_event->'error',
        coalesce(p_event->'context', '{}'::jsonb)
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_log_id;
    RETURN v_log_id;
END; $body$;

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.document_spend_summary AS
SELECT
  d.id AS document_id,
  d.fingerprint,
  d.invoice_number,
  d.total_amount,
  d.currency,
  d.received_at,
  v.id AS vendor_id,
  v.name AS vendor_name,
  v.domain AS vendor_domain,
  COUNT(li.id) AS line_item_count,
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN li.total_price > 0 THEN li.total_price ELSE 0 END), 0) AS positive_spend,
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN li.total_price < 0 THEN li.total_price ELSE 0 END), 0) AS negative_offsets
FROM public.documents d
JOIN public.vendors v ON v.id = d.vendor_id
LEFT JOIN public.line_items li ON li.document_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, v.id;

parameters.content:
## Process invoices and jobs

Handles full invoice processing and manages pipeline jobs functions.

### How it works

1. Manually trigger the setup process to start the workflow.
2. Set up necessary extensions and triggers using SQL queries.
3. Initialize various database tables and views required for the setup.
4. Execute functions to process invoices and manage pipeline jobs.
5. Finalize the setup with custom code execution to complete the workflow.

## Manual trigger setup

Starts the setup process manually.

## Finalize setup

Executes custom code to finalize the setup.

## Setup database schema

Sequentially sets up extensions, enums, and initializes tables and views.

## SETUP
run once to setup your database

### How it works

1. The workflow starts by downloading an email attachment.
2. The attachment is then uploaded to LlamaCloud for AI processing.
3. The workflow polls the AI service until extraction is complete.
4. Once completed, structured data is retrieved and checked for an invoice number.
5. If the invoice number is missing, the workflow stops with an error, otherwise, database preparation is initiated.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure access to the AI extraction service at https://api.cloud.llamaindex.ai
- [ ] Ensure email download API access to https://api.agentmail.to
- [ ] Set up a Redis instance for session management (Optional)

## Trigger and attachment download

Starts the workflow and downloads the email attachment.

## AI upload and start extraction

Uploads the PDF to LlamaCloud and initiates AI extraction.

## Polling and status check

Polls for the AI extraction status and handles the loop logic.

## Structured data handling

Handles fetched structured data and checks for invoice existence.

## Error handling or prepare database

Conditional error handling or data preparation for database storage.

## Standalone Redis check

Independent redis check for beta testing.

## MAIN 
Publish to run

## parseInvoice
THE subworkflow trigger by the mainworkflow

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Execute Workflow node can be used when you want your workflow to treat another workflow as a step in your flow. It allows you to modularize your workflows and have a single source of truth for series of actions you perform often.
